payload.title: First Mutual Transportation Assurance Company 2024 Annual Board Meeting
payload.description: FMTAC Board Book containing Annual Meeting Newsletter, Financial Statements, Actuarial Certification, Regulatory Checklist, Investment Report, Service Provider information, Disaster Response Plan, Business Continuity Plan, and Glossary for the 2024 annual FMTAC Board meeting.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: MTA's wholly owned captive insurance company formed December 5, 1997 under NY Insurance Law Section 7005, Article 70. Provides insurance coverage to MTA and its family of agencies.

warnings: entity.parent_entity is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Parent entities are relation context. Suggested relation: part_of_agency.

payload.description: Global top seventh ranked captive management business providing technical solutions to captives. Captive manager adviser for FMTAC, c/o Alliant Insurance Services.
Investment manager for FMTAC's portfolios, managing multiple trust accounts including FMTAC Ace American Trust, Excess Loss Fund, General Operating Account, etc.
Part of Oliver Wyman Group, a business unit of Marsh McLennan. Provides actuarial consulting services to FMTAC. Over 300 credentialed actuaries.
Parent entity and owner of FMTAC. A component unit of the State of New York.

tool_args.label: New York State Department of Financial Services

payload.entity_name: New York State Department of Financial Services
payload.description: Regulator of captive insurance companies in New York State.
The 2024 Board of Directors of First Mutual Transportation Assurance Company held at 2 Broadway, 20th Floor, New York, NY on May 20, 2024.
First Mutual Transportation Assurance Company was incorporated under NY laws as a pure captive insurance company and commenced operations on this date.

MTA LIRR track equipment derailed fouling live track and was struck by a train carrying passengers near New Hyde Park Station. 72 claims related to this accident: 57 passenger injuries, 8 employee injuries, property damage. $11 million FMTAC Force Account retention limit paid. $8.6 million impacted ELF program.

tool_args.label: LIRR Atlantic Terminal Bumper Block Strike

payload.description: MTA LIRR Far Rockaway Line train struck a bumper block in Atlantic Terminal-Brooklyn Station. 122 claims put into suit. $11 million FMTAC Station Maintenance retention limit paid. $19.57 million impacted. 6 open lawsuits.

Plaintiff rode bicycle through cordoned-off construction site beneath elevated subway line and was struck by a rotted cross tie. Jury awarded $110 million, reduced to roughly $69 million after post-trial motions. Case under appeal. Reported to ELF.
Mass shooting on Manhattan-bound N train between 59th Street and 36th Street subway stations in Brooklyn. 8 claims received, 4 lawsuits filed. Cases consolidated for discovery. Reported to ELF.
FMTAC Premium-to-Surplus ratio of .8:1 in 2023, operating well within industry recommended range of 5:1 or lower.
Reserve-to-Surplus ratio of 2.4:1 in 2023, within industry recommended range of 10:1 or lower.
Combined Loss & Expense Ratio of 70% in 2023.

payload.claim_text: The Company's December 31, 2023 reserves meet the relevant requirements of the insurance laws of New York, are consistent with reserves computed in accordance with accepted loss reserving standards, and make a reasonable provision for all unpaid loss and loss adjustment expense obligations.
payload.statement: Reserves meet NY insurance law, accepted loss reserving standards, and make reasonable provision for unpaid obligations.
payload.subject: FMTAC loss and loss adjustment expense reserves

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

payload.claim_text: There is a significant risk of material adverse deviation from the carried net reserve amounts. Materiality standard of 15% of total capital and surplus, or $42,789,615.
payload.statement: Significant risk of material adverse deviation exists for FMTAC's carried net reserves.
payload.subject: FMTAC carried net reserves

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

payload.claim_text: There is currently a $70 million property claim associated with damage caused by Hurricane Ida in 2021. This claim is 100% reinsured.
payload.subject: Hurricane Ida property claim
payload.statement: $70 million property claim from Hurricane Ida, 100% reinsured.

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.
